clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for medication allergy review anesthesia.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Anesthesia / Perioperative Medicine documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct high-caution documentation workflow for clinician-stated assessment and plans around medication allergy review anesthesia, without generating management advice.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
red_flag_prompts: airway, aspiration, allergy, bleeding, or cardiorespiratory risk documented if assessed
consent/risk discussion documented only if completed by clinician
perioperative concern documented without management advice

chips.text: Medication allergy review anesthesia interval history documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
change since last review documented
severity/impact on function documented if discussed
associated symptoms reviewed if relevant
relevant negatives documented if assessed
patient concerns or goals documented if discussed
clinician-led risk context documented
scope limited to documentation scaffolding
Pre/perioperative assessment documentation documented only if assessed

Cardiorespiratory assessment documentation documented only if assessed

airway, aspiration, allergy, bleeding, or cardiorespiratory risk documented if assessed

perioperative concern documented without management advice

clinician-entered documentation plan recorded
senior/specialist discussion documented if already performed
risk/consent discussion documented if clinician completed it
handover or follow-up documentation recorded if arranged by clinician

Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Existing investigations documented.

clinical_blockers: Specific medicine, reaction phenotype, timing, severity, verification, testing, de-labelling, cross-reactivity, substitute, and emergency management remain unsupported.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.
